slides.title: The Keyboard Challenge ã´ÿ¡
slides.content:
# Keyboard Covers Inputs

On mobile, the keyboard can cover the input you're typing into, especially if it's at the bottom of the screen.

React Native provides components to handle this: `KeyboardAvoidingView` and `ScrollView` with keyboard handling.

slides.title: KeyboardAvoidingView
ScrollView with Keyboard
slides.content:
## Automatically Adjusting Scroll

If your form is in a `ScrollView`, you can use:

```jsx
<ScrollView
  contentContainerStyle={{ flexGrow: 1 }}
  keyboardShouldPersistTaps="handled"
  keyboardDismissMode="on-drag"
>
  {/* form */}
</ScrollView>
```

- `keyboardShouldPersistTaps` ã whether taps should dismiss the keyboard (`'never'`, `'always'`, `'handled'`).
- `keyboardDismissMode` ã `'none'`, `'on-drag'` (dismiss when you start scrolling).

quizData.question: What is the purpose of `KeyboardAvoidingView`?

options.text: To prevent the keyboard from opening
To adjust the layout so inputs aren't hidden by the keyboard

quizData.explanation: `KeyboardAvoidingView` moves its children to avoid the onãscreen keyboard.

slides.title: Dismissing the Keyboard
slides.content:
## Programmatic Dismissal

You can dismiss the keyboard anywhere with `Keyboard.dismiss()`.

```jsx
import { Keyboard } from 'react-native';

// Dismiss on button press
<Button title="Done" onPress={() => Keyboard.dismiss()} />

// Dismiss when tapping outside inputs ã wrap your screen in a
// TouchableWithoutFeedback that calls Keyboard.dismiss.
```

Example:
```jsx
<TouchableWithoutFeedback onPress={Keyboard.dismiss}>
  <View style={{ flex: 1 }}>
    {/* inputs */}
  </View>
</TouchableWithoutFeedback>
```

slides.title: Pro Tip Þ
slides.content: Use `Keyboard.dismiss` in the `onSubmitEditing` of the last field to dismiss after submit, improving UX.
